introSection: Wilde Hilde is a steel 4th Dimension roller coaster located at Schwaben Park in Kaisersbach, Baden Wuerttemberg, Germany. Opened in 2018, it is a prototype Roller Ball model by Ride Engineers Switzerland, and it has a chicken coop theme.

sections.title: History
sections.content:
Vertical construction began in July 2018.

In September 2018, it was announced that the project would be delayed until 2019. However, a couple weeks later, it was announced that the ride's opening would be pushed forward to 13 October of that year.

In October 2025 the park announced that the coaster would be removed, saying that it was too large and frightening for its core audience of families with young children. It will be sent to another park in Germany.

sections.title: Design
sections.content: The theme is a nod to the origin of Schwaben Park, which started with a chicken coop.

fullText:
Wilde Hilde is a steel 4th Dimension roller coaster located at Schwaben Park in Kaisersbach, Baden Wuerttemberg, Germany. Opened in 2018, it is a prototype Roller Ball model by Ride Engineers Switzerland, and it has a chicken coop theme.

Vertical construction began in July 2018.

In September 2018, it was announced that the project would be delayed until 2019. However, a couple weeks later, it was announced that the ride's opening would be pushed forward to 13 October of that year.

In October 2025 the park announced that the coaster would be removed, saying that it was too large and frightening for its core audience of families with young children. It will be sent to another park in Germany.

The theme is a nod to the origin of Schwaben Park, which started with a chicken coop.

Riders sit facing back-to-back.
